summary.intent: Hide checkpoints that have no associated events from the UI, so users only see meaningful checkpoints.
summary.outcome: Checkpoints with no events are filtered in two places: the SessionDetail sidebar (client-side using already-loaded event data) and the BranchLog server endpoint (server-side via a database existence check before sending data to the client).

repo: Checkpoint event data is pre-grouped in SessionDetail using a `groups` map keyed by checkpointId, making client-side filtering cheap.
The BranchLog endpoint in src/server.ts constructs entries via a sessionLink → checkpointMetadata join, allowing a findFirst existence check to gate inclusion.
Filtering is applied at both the server and client layers for this feature — server skips entries before sending, client filters the sidebar display.

code.finding: Sidebar checkpoints filtered with `groups.get(cp.checkpointId)?.length > 0` using the pre-loaded groups map — no extra data fetching needed.
BranchLog endpoint uses a Prisma findFirst with a sessionLink → checkpointMetadata join to check for linked log events before pushing each entry.

workflow: When filtering can be done client-side using already-loaded data, prefer that over adding server round-trips.
For list endpoints, server-side filtering before serialization is cleaner than relying solely on client filtering.
